label: 11_palestinian_palestinians_hamas_israeli
description: The Israeli-Palestinian conflict intensifies in the West Bank as violence and land seizures escalate. Analysts argue that settlement expansion is a major obstacle to peace, while scholars propose ideas for addressing key issues ahead of an election season.

earliest_articles.title: The Ghosts of Negotiations Past: How Palestinians are Applying Past Lessons to the Current Peace Process

newest_articles.title: What could the Israel-Gaza deal mean for the Middle East?
newest_articles.excerpt: On October 8, 2025, Israel and Hamas agreed to a U.S. ceasefire proposal regarding the war in the Gaza Strip. Below, Brookings scholars reflect on the agreement and what it means for the broader Middle Eastern region.
